title: dorycnium pentaphyllum var. insulare
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. insulare, also known as the Cyprus annual knapweed or Island tickle-weed, is a variety of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. insulare include its ability to support digestive health, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. insulare can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and benefits.
